name: Trails steeped in history in Escornalbou – hiking in Catalonia
name_original: Caminos empapados de historia en Escornalbou
description:
The Escornalbou barony brought together various towns in the Sierra de l'Argentera, around the castle and monastery of Sant Miquel d'Escornalbou. Founded in 1153, the monastery underwent a spectacular and controversial restoration at the beginning of the 20th century. It was from the hand of the Indian and philanthropist Eduard Toda. On this Route through cobbled paths I suggest a walk through two of the barony's towns, l'Argentera and Duesaigües, as well as a visit to the unique castle and monastery of Sant Miquel d'Escornalbou.

You start the journey in l'Argentera, a picturesque town known for its cobbled streets and the flowers that decorate its houses. As soon as you leave the town you begin the ascent to the castle, which can be visited throughout the year. You go up the cobbled path that the monks used to go to and from l'Argentera. The castle promontory has a hermitage at its highest point from which you can enjoy spectacular views ranging from the Prades mountains and the Montsant peaks to the north, to, on a clear day, the Ebro Delta or even the Tramuntana mountains in Majorca.

After leaving the castle, you take the road to Duesaigües, which runs through an oak grove and where you find the Vilamanya fountain, donated to the town by Eduard Toda. Shortly before reaching Duesaigües, you come across the laundry, built at the end of the 19th century which, in addition to collecting rainwater, has a pond to store irrigation water. The last section of the Route follows the Camino Real until it returns to l'Argentera.
region_intro: Hiking around L'Argentera offers diverse trails across a landscape characterized by scrubland, extensive pine and oak forests, and ravines. The region, situated in Catalonia, Spain, features a mountainous interior with views extending towards the Mediterranean Sea. Trails range from easy village loops to moderate paths exploring historical sites. The terrain includes both gentle slopes and more varied ascents, providing options for different fitness levels. Best hiking trails around L'Argentera The most popular hiking route is Trails steeped in history in Escornalbou – hiking in Catalonia, a 4.9 miles (7.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 34 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores historical sites near Escornalbou castle. Another top favourite among local hikers is L'Argentera Village – L'Argentera loop from l'Argentera, a moderate 4.2 miles (6.8 km) path. This loop offers a journey through the village surroundings and local forests. Local hikers also love the Paved path – The Puigferrós Viewpoint loop from l'Argentera, a 2.8 miles (4.5 km) trail leading through varied terrain to a scenic viewpoint, often completed in about 1 hour 39 minutes. Hiking around L'Argentera is defined by its forests, ravines, and historical landmarks, offering picturesque views of the Baix Camp region. The network provides options for easy walks and more challenging moderate routes, suitable for various ability levels. The routes in L'Argentera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 400 reviews. More than 2000 hikers have used komoot to explore L'Argentera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of hiking trails can I find in L'Argentera, Catalonia?
A: L'Argentera in Catalonia offers a diverse range of trails, from easy village loops through pine and oak forests to moderate paths exploring historical sites. The terrain includes gentle slopes and more varied ascents, suitable for different fitness levels. You'll find paths that wind through scrubland, ravines, and offer picturesque views of the Baix Camp region.

Q: Are there any easy hiking routes suitable for beginners or families in L'Argentera?
A: Yes, L'Argentera has several easy routes perfect for beginners or families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10419595" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paved path – The Puigferrós Viewpoint loop from l'Argentera&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.5 km trail that takes about 1 hour 39 minutes. Another great option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33694117" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Enchanted Gate loop from l'Argentera&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 4.2 km hike lasting about 1 hour 21 minutes.

Q: What historical landmarks or points of interest can I explore while hiking in L'Argentera?
A: The area around L'Argentera is rich in history. You can explore trails steeped in history near Escornalbou castle, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1201468936/trails-steeped-in-history-in-escornalbou-hiking-in-catalonia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trails steeped in history in Escornalbou – hiking in Catalonia&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable historical sites include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5137425" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Miquel d'Escornalbou Castle and Monastery&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/345742" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Escornalbou Castle-Monastery&lt;/a&gt;, offering expansive views of the region.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in L'Argentera?
A: Yes, L'Argentera features several convenient circular routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10419575" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;L'Argentera Village – L'Argentera loop from l'Argentera&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 6.8 km path that takes you through the village surroundings and local forests. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10419594" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;L'Argentera – L'Argentera Village loop from l'Argentera&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 6.4 km route.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in L'Argentera?
A: The hiking routes in L'Argentera are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 400 reviews. Hikers often praise the picturesque views of the Baix Camp region, the well-maintained paths through forests and ravines, and the variety of options for different ability levels.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in L'Argentera?
A: Given its Mediterranean climate, L'Argentera in Catalonia is generally pleasant for hiking during spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) when temperatures are milder. Summer can be hot, especially at midday, while winter offers cooler, crisp conditions, though some higher elevation paths might be less accessible.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes in the broader L'Argentera region?
A: While L'Argentera in Catalonia primarily offers easy to moderate trails, the broader Mercantour-Argentera Massif in the Maritime Alps (bordering France and Italy) is renowned for its challenging high-alpine hikes. These routes often involve significant elevation gains, require good fitness, sure-footedness, and sometimes alpine experience, with some summit ascents requiring climbing skills.

Q: Are there any natural monuments or scenic viewpoints accessible via hiking trails?
A: Yes, L'Argentera offers several natural monuments and scenic viewpoints. You can reach the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2375269" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mont-Redon Summit&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/615048" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Miranda (918 m)&lt;/a&gt; for panoramic views. The region itself provides picturesque scenery with olive and hazelnut fields, and mountain views that extend towards the Mediterranean Sea.

Q: How many hiking trails are available in L'Argentera?
A: There are over 10 hiking routes available in L'Argentera, ranging from easy to moderate difficulty. This includes 4 easy routes, 5 moderate routes, and a few more challenging options, ensuring there's something for every hiker.

Q: Is L'Argentera suitable for mountain biking or other outdoor activities besides hiking?
A: Beyond hiking, the L'Argentera region in Catalonia is also suitable for other outdoor activities such as mountain biking, road cycling, and horseback riding, taking advantage of its diverse terrain and scenic landscapes.

Q: What is the typical elevation gain on hikes in L'Argentera?
A: Hikes in L'Argentera, Catalonia, typically feature moderate elevation gains. For example, a moderate route like the 'Trails steeped in history in Escornalbou' has an elevation gain of around 334 meters (1,096 feet), while easier loops might have gains of 190-270 meters (620-880 feet).
tips: This old road connects the convent of Sant Miquel d'Escornalbou with the town of Argentera. It is part of the network of roads in the barony of Escornalbou, whose paved roads are being restored and signposted.

The Castillo Monasterio de Escornalbou de Riudecanyes is a stately mansion from the beginning of the 20th century formed by the remains of two medieval buildings: the castle, built on the remains of a Roman fortress, and the monastery of Sant Miquel.

Located halfway between the town of Duesaigües and the castle-monastery of Sant Miquel d'Escornalbou, this fountain was "donated" to the town by the philanthropist Eduard Toda in 1925, also paying for driving to the town. Until the end of the 20th century, the source provided water to the town of Desaigües.

The Duesaigües laundries were built at the end of the 19th century and restored in 1995. It has two roofs sloping towards the basins, which allows the recovery of rainwater. It also has a pond to store excess water and channel it later for irrigation.
